Containers for oopr Instances

oopr_containers.Rd

Create a vector or key-value pair of oopr instances.

Usage

OoprVec(ooprC)

OoprMap(ooprC)

# S3 method for class 'OoprVec'
x[i, j, ..., drop]

# S3 method for class 'OoprVec'
x[i, j, ...] <- value

# S3 method for class 'OoprMap'
x[i, j, ..., drop]

# S3 method for class 'OoprMap'
x[i, j, ...] <- value

Arguments

ooprC

ooprC
An oopr class.

OoprVec

Fields

class

character(1L)
The name of the underlying oopr class.

empty

logical(1L)
Whether there are no instanced classes in the container.

size

integer(1L)
The amount of instanced classes in the container.

data

list()
The container.

Methods

insert

Insert an already instanced class.

emplace

Construct a new class into the container.

resize

Pre-allocate or destroy the container.

erase

Remove a class from the container.

swap

Swap two elements of the container.

apply

Apply a function over every element in the container.

[

Access an element of the container.

[<-

Assign to an element of the container.

insert

Description

Insert an already instanced class.

Usage


insert(pos = this$size, x)

Arguments

posinteger(1L)
The index to insert the class. The default is at the back.
xoopr
An oopr instance of class $class.

Returns

this invisibly.

emplace

Description

Construct a new class into the container.

Usage


emplace(. = this$size, ...)

Arguments

.integer(1L)
As per pos argument for the $insert method.
...varies
Arguments to pass to $class's constructor.

Returns

this invisibly.

resize

Description

Pre-allocate or destroy the container.

Usage


resize(n)

Arguments

ninteger(1L)
The desired length to make the container.

Returns

this invisibly.

erase

Description

Remove a class from the container.

Usage


erase(pos = this$size)

Arguments

posinteger(1L)
The index in the container to remove.

Returns

this invisibly.

swap

Description

Swap two elements of the container.

Usage


swap(pos1, pos2)

Arguments

pos1integer(1L)
Element to swap.
pos2integer(1L)
Element to swap.

Returns

this invisibly.

apply

Description

Apply a function over every element in the container.

Usage


apply(fun, ...)

Arguments

funfunction
Function to use, the first argument not in ... will be the containers class instances.
...varies
Further arguments to fun.

Returns

list() of the outputs from fun.

[

Description

Access an element of the container.

Usage


`[`(i, j, ..., drop)

Arguments

iinteger(1L)
The element to access.
jNot Used
...Not Used
dropNot Used

Returns

An oopr object of class $class.

[<-

Description

Assign to an element of the container.

Usage


`[<-`(i, j, ..., value)

Arguments

iinteger(1L)
The element to assign to.
jNot Used
...Not Used
valueoopr
An oopr object of class $class.

Details

Can be used to set members of classes within the container, e.g. x[i]$mem <- ....

Returns

this invisibly.

OoprMap

Fields

class

character(1L)
The name of the underlying oopr class.

empty

logical(1L)
Whether there are no instanced classes in the container.

size

integer(1L)
The amount of instanced classes in the container.

keys

character()
The keys within the container

data

list()
The container.

Methods

exists

Check whether a key exists.

insert

Insert an already instanced class.

emplace

Construct a new class into the container.

erase

Remove a class from the container.

resize

Pre-allocate or destroy the container.

apply

Apply a function over every element in the container.

[

Access an element of the container.

[<-

Assign to an element of the container.

exists

Description

Check whether a key exists.

Usage


exists(key)

Arguments

keycharacter(1L)
The key to check for

Returns

logical(1L).

insert

Description

Insert an already instanced class.

Usage


insert(key, x)

Arguments

keycharacter(1L)
The index to insert the class. The default is at the back.
xoopr
An oopr instance of class $class.

Returns

this invisibly.

emplace

Description

Construct a new class into the container.

Usage


emplace(., ...)

Arguments

.character(1L)
As per key argument for the $insert method.
...varies
Arguments to pass to $class's constructor.

Returns

this invisibly.

erase

Description

Remove a class from the container.

Usage


erase(key)

Arguments

keycharacter(1L)
The index in the container to remove.

Returns

this invisibly.

resize

Description

Pre-allocate or destroy the container.

Usage


resize(keys = character(0L))

Arguments

keyscharacter()
Keys to create / remain.

Returns

this invisibly.

apply

Description

Apply a function over every element in the container.

Usage


apply(fun, ...)

Arguments

funfunction
Function to apply with, the first two arguments being the key and value, respectively.
...varies
Further arguments to fun.

Returns

list() of the outputs from fun.

[

Description

Access an element of the container.

Usage


`[`(i, j, ..., drop)

Arguments

icharacter(1L)
The element to access.
jNot Used
...Not Used
dropNot Used

Returns

An oopr object of class $class.

[<-

Description

Assign to an element of the container.

Usage


`[<-`(i, j, ..., value)

Arguments

icharacter(1L)
The element to assign to.
jNot Used
...Not Used
valueoopr
An oopr object of class $class.

Details

Can be used to set members of classes within the container, e.g. x[i]$mem <- ....

Returns

this invisibly.

Examples

## ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ##
oopr("test",,
{
test <- \(x) { this$x <- x; }
public:x <- 0L;
})
## ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ##
# create a vector
vec <- OoprVec(test);

# $emplace create a new instance
vec$emplace(, 1L);

# $insert passes existing instance
vec$insert(0L, test(2L));

print(vec);
#> <OoprVec: 0x55edd7bde070>
#>  ├─$class  : chr "test"
#>  ├─$empty  : logi FALSE
#>  ├─$size   : int 2
#>  ├─$data   :List of 2
#>  │           $:<test: 0x55edd7fcffd0>
#>  │            ..└─$x: int 2
#>  │           $:<test: 0x55edd7f8b178>
#>  │            ..└─$x: int 1
#>  ├─$insert :\(pos = this$size, x)  
#>  ├─$emplace:\(. = this$size, x)  
#>  ├─$resize :\(n)  
#>  ├─$erase  :\(pos = this$size)  
#>  ├─$swap   :\(pos1, pos2)  
#>  ├─$apply  :\(fun, ...)  
#>  ├─$[      :\(i, j, ..., drop)  
#>  └─$[<-    :\(i, j, ..., value)  

# $swap will swap elements
vec$swap(2L, 1L);
print(vec);
#> <OoprVec: 0x55edd7bde070>
#>  ├─$class  : chr "test"
#>  ├─$empty  : logi FALSE
#>  ├─$size   : int 2
#>  ├─$data   :List of 2
#>  │           $:<test: 0x55edd7f8b178>
#>  │            ..└─$x: int 1
#>  │           $:<test: 0x55edd7fcffd0>
#>  │            ..└─$x: int 2
#>  ├─$insert :\(pos = this$size, x)  
#>  ├─$emplace:\(. = this$size, x)  
#>  ├─$resize :\(n)  
#>  ├─$erase  :\(pos = this$size)  
#>  ├─$swap   :\(pos1, pos2)  
#>  ├─$apply  :\(fun, ...)  
#>  ├─$[      :\(i, j, ..., drop)  
#>  └─$[<-    :\(i, j, ..., value)  

# $apply to loop over instances
vec$apply(\(x) { x$x <- x$x + 1L; })
#> [[1]]
#> [1] 2
#> 
#> [[2]]
#> [1] 3
#> 
vec$apply(\(x) { x$x; })
#> [[1]]
#> [1] 2
#> 
#> [[2]]
#> [1] 3
#> 
## ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ ##
# create a key-value pair
map <- OoprMap(test);
map$emplace("a", "a")$emplace("b", "b");
print(map);
#> <OoprMap: 0x55edd887fe40>
#>  ├─$class  : chr "test"
#>  ├─$empty  : logi FALSE
#>  ├─$size   : int 2
#>  ├─$keys   : chr [1:2] "a" "b"
#>  ├─$data   :List of 2
#>  │           $a:<test: 0x55edd88ab750>
#>  │            ..└─$x: chr "a"
#>  │           $b:<test: 0x55edd88aebf0>
#>  │            ..└─$x: chr "b"
#>  ├─$exists :\(key)  
#>  ├─$insert :\(key, x)  
#>  ├─$emplace:\(., x)  
#>  ├─$erase  :\(key)  
#>  ├─$resize :\(keys = character(0L))  
#>  ├─$apply  :\(fun, ...)  
#>  ├─$[      :\(i, j, ..., drop)  
#>  └─$[<-    :\(i, j, ..., value)  

# apply is a two argument function
map$apply(\(k, o) { o$x <- toupper(k); });
#> $a
#> [1] "A"
#> 
#> $b
#> [1] "B"
#> 
map$apply(\(k, o) { o$x == k; });
#> $a
#> [1] FALSE
#> 
#> $b
#> [1] FALSE
#>

On this page
